title: Cyclosorus obstructus
intro: Cyclosorus obstructus, also known as the Chinese Leptosorus or Common Vase Fern, is a species of fern in the family Thelypteridaceae. This plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine, particularly in Chinese and Southeast Asian folk remedies, to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues, due to its anti-inflammatory, antipyretic, and antioxidant properties. The bioactive constituents of Cyclosorus obstructus include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins, which contribute to its medicinal effects. In traditional herbal medicine, the plant is often prepared as a tea, decoction, or dried powder, and is also used in modern herbal supplements to promote overall health and well-being.
taxonomy: Cyclosorus obstructus is classified within the plant family Thelypteridaceae, which is a group of ferns. This fern is native to tropical and subtropical regions, including parts of Asia and the Pacific. Cyclosorus obstructus belongs to the genus Cyclosorus, a group of ferns known for their distinctive leaf morphology. The species is further classified as a member of the order Polypodiales, which includes a wide range of fern species. By understanding its classification, researchers can better study the evolutionary history and relationships of Cyclosorus obstructus.
